sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, presented with neck pain following multiple car crashes, with the most recent incident occurring in February of this year.
He reports experiencing sharp, incapacitating pain that prevents him from working, accompanied by headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
He has been experiencing fatigue since the accident.
He has a history of previous car crashes, including one that required surgery, and has been to therapy and a pain clinic.
He has been prescribed fentanyl in the past but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including one that required surgery.
He has also been to therapy and a pain clinic.
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, dizziness, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the bony process and muscle in the neck, with limited range of motion, including difficulty bending the neck forward and sideways.
No bruising or swelling is noted.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, with pain explained by multiple factors, but no fracture.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Suspected cervical spine injury; Fatigue; Numbness in left arm and right leg; Spasms throughout body; Headaches; Dizziness.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500 milligrams every 6 to 8 hours for 6 to 8 hours, to help relax the muscles in the area and alleviate pain.
I recommended that the patient apply ice to the affected area and perform exercises to improve range of motion.
I also ordered an MRI to further evaluate the extent of the injury.
The patient will be referred to physical therapy and pain management if necessary.
The patient will be given a note excusing him from work until the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen after the MRI results are available to determine the next course of treatment.
